title: Find a missing side length when given perimeter
description: Find a missing side length for a figure when given the perimeter.
topic_path: Find a missing side length when given perimeter

assessment_items.question_content:
The regular pentagon below has a perimeter of $55\text{ cm}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the length of one side of the pentagon?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

Perimeter is the sum of all the side lengths. 



[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\,\greenD?=\purpleC{55}$

Since a *regular* pentagon has $\blueD5$ sides of equal length, we can also multiply:


$\begin{align} \purpleC{\text{Perimeter}} &= \blueD5\times\, \greenD? \\
\\
\purpleC{55} &= \blueD5\times\,\greenD? \end{align}$

We divide to find the side length.

$\purpleC{55}\div \blueD5= \,\greenD?$
The length of one side of the pentagon is $\greenD{11\text{ cm}}$.

options.alt: A five-sided figure with sides of equal length. One side is labeled with a question mark centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The triangle below has a perimeter of $27\text{ cm}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the length of one side of the triangle?**  
*All $3$ sides are the same length.*

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{cm}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

Perimeter is the sum of all the side lengths. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\,\greenD?=\purpleC{27}$

Since an *equilateral* triangle has $\blueD3$ sides of equal length, we can also multiply:


$\begin{align} \purpleC{\text{Perimeter}} &= \blueD3\times\, \greenD? \\
\\
\purpleC{27} &= \blueD3\times\,\greenD? \end{align}$

We divide to find the side length.

$\purpleC{27}\div \blueD3= \,\greenD?$
The length of one side of the triangle is $\greenD{9\text{ cm}}$.

options.alt: A triangle with sides of equal length. One side is labeled question mark centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The square below has a perimeter of $12\text{ m}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the length of one side of the square?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] $\text{m}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

Perimeter is the sum of all the side lengths. 



[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\greenD? =\purpleC{12}$

Since a square has $\blueD{4}$ sides of equal length, we can also multiply:


$\begin{align} \purpleC{\text{Perimeter}} &= \blueD{4}\times\, \greenD? \\
\\
\purpleC{12} &= \blueD{4}\times\,\greenD? \end{align}$

We divide to find the side length.

$\purpleC{12}\div \blueD{4}= \,\greenD?$
The length of one side of the square is $\greenD{3\text{ m}}$.

options.alt: A square with one side labeled question mark meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of the rectangle is $40\text{ m}$.   

[[☃ image 1]]

 **What is the length of the missing side?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

The total perimeter is $40\,\text{m}$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{13} + \purpleC{?} + \greenD{13}  + \purpleC{?} = {40\,\text{m}}$

The sum of all the known lengths is:

$\greenD{13} + \greenD{13} = {26\,\text{m}}$


Now, we subtract the known lengths from  $40\,\text{m}$ to find the unknown part of the perimeter.  

$40- {26} = \redC{14\,\text{ m}}$

Now we have:

$\purpleC{?} + \purpleC{?} = \redC{14}$

Because we have $\blueD{2}$ sides that are both $\purpleC{?\,\text{m}}$ long, we can multiply.

$\blueD{2} \times \purpleC{?} = \redC{14}$

We can divide to find the side length.

$\redC{14} \div \blueD{2} = \purpleC{?}$
The missing side length is  $\purpleC{7\,\text{m}}$.

options.alt: A rectangle with a length of 13 meters and a width labeled question mark meters.

answers.message: This is twice the length.  Divide by 2.

$\greenD{4} + \greenD{4} = {8\,\text{m}}$


Now, we subtract the known lengths from  $26\,\text{m}$ to find the unknown part of the perimeter.  

$26- {8} = \redC{18\,\text{ m}}$

Now we have:

$\purpleC{?} + \purpleC{?} = \redC{18}$

Because we have $\blueD{2}$ sides that are both $\purpleC{?\,\text{m}}$ long, we can multiply.

$\blueD{2} \times \purpleC{?} = \redC{18}$

We can divide to find the side length.

$\redC{18} \div \blueD{2} = \purpleC{?}$
The missing side length is  $\purpleC{9\,\text{m}}$.

options.alt: A rectangle with a length of 4 meters and a width labeled question mark meters.

answers.message: This is twice the length.  Divide by 2.

assessment_items.question_content:
A square has a perimeter of $48 \text{ cm}$.

**What is the length of one side of the square?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

Perimeter is the sum of all the side lengths. 



[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\,\greenD?=\purpleC{48}$

Since a square has $\blueD4$ sides of equal length, we can also multiply:


$\begin{align} \purpleC{\text{Perimeter}} &= \blueD4\times\, \greenD? \\
\\
\purpleC{48} &= \blueD4\times\,\greenD? \end{align}$

We divide to find the side length.

$\purpleC{48}\div \blueD4= \,\greenD?$
The length of one side of the square is $\greenD{12\text{ cm}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A triangle has a perimeter of $24\text{ m}$.  All $3$ sides are the same length.

**What is the length of one side of the triangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]  $\text{m}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

Perimeter is the sum of all the side lengths. 


[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD?+\,\greenD?+\greenD?=\purpleC{24}$

Since the triangle has $\blueD{3}$ sides of equal length, we can also multiply:


$\begin{align} \purpleC{\text{Perimeter}} &= \blueD{3}\times\, \greenD? \\
\\
\purpleC{24} &= \blueD{3}\times\,\greenD? \end{align}$

We divide to find the side length.

$\purpleC{24}\div \blueD{3}= \,\greenD?$
The length of one side of the triangle is $\greenD{8\text{ m}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of the rectangle is $32\text{ m}$.   One side is $11\,\text{m}$ long.

 **What is the length of the missing side?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

The total perimeter is $32\,\text{m}$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{11} + \purpleC{?} + \greenD{11}  + \purpleC{?} = {32\,\text{m}}$

The sum of all the known lengths is:

$\greenD{11} + \greenD{11} = {22\,\text{m}}$


Now, we subtract the known lengths from  $32\,\text{m}$ to find the unknown part of the perimeter.  

$32- {22} = \redC{10\,\text{ m}}$

Now we have:

$\purpleC{?} + \purpleC{?} = \redC{10}$

Because we have $\blueD{2}$ sides that are both $\purpleC{?\,\text{m}}$ long, we can multiply.

$\blueD{2} \times \purpleC{?} = \redC{10}$

We can divide to find the side length.

$\redC{10} \div \blueD{2} = \purpleC{?}$
The missing side length is  $\purpleC{5\,\text{m}}$.

answers.message: This is twice the length.  Divide by 2.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of the rectangle is $20\text{ cm}$.   One side is $6\,\text{cm}$ long.

 **What is the length of the missing side?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

The total perimeter is $20\,\text{cm}$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{6} + \purpleC{?} + \greenD{6}  + \purpleC{?} = {20\,\text{cm}}$

The sum of all the known lengths is:

$\greenD{6} + \greenD{6} = {12\,\text{cm}}$


Now, we subtract the known lengths from  $20\,\text{cm}$ to find the unknown part of the perimeter.  

$20- {12} = \redC{8\,\text{ cm}}$

Now we have:

$\purpleC{?} + \purpleC{?} = \redC{8}$

Because we have $\blueD{2}$ sides that are both $\purpleC{?\,\text{cm}}$ long, we can multiply.

$\blueD{2} \times \purpleC{?} = \redC{8}$

We can divide to find the side length.

$\redC{8} \div \blueD{2} = \purpleC{?}$
The missing side length is  $\purpleC{4\,\text{cm}}$.

answers.message: This is twice the length.  Divide by 2.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of the figure is $38\text{ cm}$.   

[[☃ image 1]]

 **What is the length of the missing side?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

[[☃ image 1]]

The sum of all the known lengths is:

$\maroonD{10} + \blueD4 + \greenD6 + \purpleC5 + \gray9 = {34\,\text{cm}}$.

The total perimeter is $38\,\text{cm}$.  

Subtract the known lengths from  $38\,\text{cm}$ to find the missing length.  

$38 - {34} = \redC{4\,\text{ cm}}$
The missing side length is  $\redC{4\,\text{cm}}$.

options.alt: A six-sided figure with sides labeled as follows: 10 centimeters, 4 centimeters, 6 centimeters, 5 centimeters, question mark centimeters, and 9 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of the figure is $31\text{ m}$.   

[[☃ image 1]]

 **What is the length of the missing side?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$
hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.

[[☃ image 1]]

The sum of all the known lengths is:
$\redC8 + \blueD6 + \purpleC{10}  = \maroonD{{24}\,\text{m}}$.

The total perimeter is $31\,\text{m}$.  

Subtract the known lengths from  $31\,\text{m}$ to find the missing length.  

$31 - \maroonD{24} = \greenD{7\text{ m}}$
The missing side length is  $\greenD {7\,\text{m}}$.

 **What is the length of the missing side?**

hints: The distance around a closed figure is called its perimeter.
